info.name: Luke Platform — Master
info.description:
Everything built in luke-0531: auth-engine (gateway+translator), core-engine (CIBSeven identity/roles/topics/process), capability-engine (capabilities + per-user access + Forms).

RUN ORDER: run top-to-bottom. Folder 0 sets up identity + FORMS access; folder 1 'Dev token' captures a gateway act-as token into {{token}} (reused by all later Bearer calls — one token covers a full run). If a single request 401s after ~60s, re-run folder 1 'Dev token'.

ASSUMES the local dev stack:
  • auth-engine :8083  LUKE_AUTH_DEV_MODE=true, LUKE_AUTH_SERVICE_KEYS=s3cret-orderbot=svc:order-bot
  • core-engine :8080  LUKE_AUTH_GATEWAY_ENABLED=true + JWKS=http://localhost:8083/.well-known/jwks.json
  • capability  :8082  LUKE_AUTH_GATEWAY_JWKS_URL=http://localhost:8083/.well-known/jwks.json
Auth per folder: Basic admin (core /api + engine-rest admin), gateway Bearer (forms/capability/user views), X-Dev-User (/session), X-Service-Key (/service/token).

item.name: 0 · Health & Setup
item.description: Run first. Brings up identity (tenant+user+role+candidate group) and FORMS access so the rest works. Idempotent. Assumes the local dev stack: auth-engine dev-mode + service key, core gateway enabled, capability verifier enabled.

item.name: 1 · Auth Engine (luke-auth-engine :8083)
item.description: Clerk-verifying gateway + translator. /session merges core roles + capability access into can[]. /dev/token and /service/token mint act-as tokens (dev / robot ingress).

item.name: 2 · Core Engine — Identity & Admin (:8080)
item.description: Custom /api identity + onboarding. /api/me/permissions rolls the 5 engine roles into 3 (tenantUser/processUser/taskUser) and is what auth-engine reads.

item.name: 3 · Core Engine — Topic Registry
item.description: External-task topic registry (worker metadata). Listed here for completeness.

item.name: 4 · Core Engine — Process API (engine-rest)
item.description: Representative CIBSeven REST reads, authenticated by the gateway Bearer (user) or Basic (admin). The full engine-rest surface is available the same way.

item.name: 5 · Capability Engine — Catalog & Subscriptions (:8082)
item.description: Global capability catalog + per-tenant subscriptions (the on/off switch).

item.name: 6 · Capability Engine — Access Grants
item.description: Per-user capability level. /api/my-capabilities is verified by the gateway token (X-User-Id is ignored).

item.name: 7 · Forms — Definitions & Versioning
item.description: Form templates: CRUD + versioning (check-in compiles, publish promotes) + resolve for the renderer/engine. Gated by FORMS read/read-write via the verified token.

item.name: 8 · Forms — Instances (runtime)
item.description: A concrete runtime occurrence: create (pins published version) → send → save → submit → processed, with the lifecycle state machine.

item.name: 9 · Forms — Retire & Trash
item.description: Lifecycle end: retire keeps it resolvable; delete trashes; purge removes. Purge last — cleans up the test form.
